name: github
description: Browserless GitHub operations implemented directly through GitHub REST and GraphQL requests, with no external CLI dependency.

auth.api_setup_scope: authenticated api.github.com REST and GraphQL with higher rate limits

typed_fast_paths: issue creation from a normal website login
authenticated REST and website GraphQL reads and writes
bounded access to every GitHub REST and GraphQL endpoint
authenticated pull request open/merge/review, issue and PR comments, issue edit/close, reactions, stars, forks, releases, workflow dispatch, and gists
coverage.website_parity: Typed commands and api_request cover GitHub REST and GraphQL directly. A normal website authorization can create issues and replay any current persisted website GraphQL operation by discovering its current query ID and verified-fetch nonce from GitHub's own assets. direct_request and direct_workflow reuse the retained session for remaining same-site operations without delegating to another CLI.
not_mapped: teams, organization membership, and branch protection
gist edit and delete after creation
human_boundaries: password, passkey, OTP, CAPTCHA, organization SSO, legal consent, and payment confirmation when GitHub requires them
constraints: GitHub authentication and authorization policies still apply
payment and other legal confirmations require the human

search_repositories.description: Search public GitHub repositories with compact metadata and rate-limit status. Without github.configure_token this uses GitHub's anonymous API quota; a github.com website login does not increase it.
search_repositories.when_to_use: Use this to find public repositories from a query and qualifiers such as language or stars.
search_repositories.when_not_to_use: Do not use this to fetch one known repository; call repository instead.

query.description: GitHub repository search query and qualifiers

repository.when_to_use: Use this to fetch metadata for one known public repository.
repository.when_not_to_use: Do not use this to search across repositories; call search_repositories.

website_graphql.when_not_to_use: Do not use this for stable reads; prefer a typed command or api_request.

api_get.description: Bounded read-only escape hatch for public GitHub REST endpoints not yet represented by a typed command. Paths stay fixed to api.github.com and only GET is allowed.
api_get.when_to_use: Use this read-only escape hatch for public REST endpoints without a typed command.
api_get.when_not_to_use: Do not use this for mutations; it is read-only. Use api_request with confirm.

configure_token.description: Validate and save a GitHub fine-grained or personal access token in the local AgentWeb profile with mode 0600. The token is never returned.
configure_token.when_to_use: Use this once to validate and store a fine-grained or personal access token for higher rate limits.
configure_token.when_not_to_use: Do not use this to authenticate the website session; that uses agentweb connect github.

token.description: GitHub fine-grained or personal access token

errors.message: GitHub rejected the provided access token.
errors.user_action: Provide a valid, unexpired token with the needed scopes and retry.

errors.message: GitHub is temporarily unavailable or returned a server error.
errors.user_action: Wait briefly and retry the same request without changing valid inputs.

disconnect.description: Delete the GitHub token stored in this AgentWeb profile. Environment-provided tokens are unchanged.
disconnect.when_to_use: Use this to delete the token stored in this profile; environment-provided tokens are unchanged.
disconnect.when_not_to_use: Do not use this to sign out the website session; it only removes the stored token.

confirm.description: Must be true to delete the stored token

errors.message: This operation changes remote state and requires confirm=true.
errors.user_action: Inspect the inputs and repeat the call with confirm set to true.

auth_status.description: Report website-session and API-token authentication separately, including API quota tier and the age/freshness of any last-known budget snapshot.
auth_status.when_to_use: Use this to report website-session and API-token authentication and quota tier separately.
auth_status.when_not_to_use: Do not use this to inspect a public user; call user.

errors.message: GitHub is temporarily unavailable or returned a server error.
errors.user_action: Wait briefly and retry the same request without changing valid inputs.

api_request.description: Call any fixed-host GitHub REST or GraphQL endpoint. GET and HEAD are read-only; POST, PUT, PATCH, and DELETE require confirm=true. Authentication is reused automatically when available.
api_request.when_to_use: Use this fixed-host REST or GraphQL bridge for reads, or for writes with confirm and a token.
api_request.when_not_to_use: Do not use this for typed reads that already have a dedicated command.

path.description: GitHub API path such as /user or /graphql

method.description: HTTP method; GET and HEAD are read-only

body.description: JSON request body; use agentweb call or MCP for nested objects

confirm.description: Required for any state-changing HTTP method

max_items.description: Maximum list items retained in the response

max_string.description: Maximum characters kept per string value

max_total_chars.description: Hard ceiling for the complete nested JSON response

set_star.description: Star or unstar a repository for the authenticated user via the REST API. Requires a token and confirm.
set_star.when_to_use: Use this to star (starred=true) or unstar (starred=false) a repo; confirm is mandatory.

starred.description: True to star, false to unstar

confirm.description: Must be true to perform the write

errors.message: This operation changes remote state and requires confirm=true.
errors.user_action: Inspect the inputs and repeat the call with confirm set to true.

errors.message: This GitHub write requires a configured API token.
errors.user_action: Run github.configure_token once with a fine-grained PAT, then retry.

errors.message: GitHub has no resource at that owner, repo, or number.
errors.user_action: Confirm the identifiers and that the token can see the resource.

errors.message: One or more arguments were empty or outside their allowed range.
errors.user_action: Fix the reported field against the input schema and call again.

errors.message: GitHub applied an API rate limit to this client.
errors.user_action: Wait for the reset, or configure a token with a higher rate limit.

errors.message: GitHub is temporarily unavailable or returned a server error.
errors.user_action: Wait briefly and retry the same request without changing inputs.

create_release.description: Create a release (optionally draft or prerelease) for a repository via the authenticated REST API. Requires a token and confirm.
create_release.when_to_use: Use this to publish or draft a release for a tag; confirm is mandatory.

dispatch_workflow.description: Trigger a workflow_dispatch run for a GitHub Actions workflow via the authenticated REST API. Requires a token and confirm.
dispatch_workflow.when_to_use: Use this to run a workflow that supports workflow_dispatch; confirm is mandatory.
dispatch_workflow.when_not_to_use: Do not use without confirm=true or for workflows without a dispatch trigger.
